QF.id: VII Market Participants in Securities Markets_3

question.text: What is the minimum net worth criterion for an individual to qualify as an Accredited Investor?

answer.text: Rs. 7.5 crore, of which at least Rs. 3.75 crore must be in financial assets
Rs. 5 crore in total net worth
Rs. 10 crore with no financial asset requirement
Rs. 2 crore in financial assets only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An individual can qualify as an Accredited Investor through any one of three criteria: (i) Annual income >= Rs. 2 crore; OR (ii) Net worth >= Rs. 7.5 crore with at least Rs. 3.75 crore in financial assets; OR (iii) Annual income >= Rs. 1 crore + Net worth >= Rs. 5 crore with at least Rs. 2.5 crore in financial assets. **The Trap:** The net worth criteria have a financial asset sub-requirement — total net worth alone is not sufficient. Option B ignores the financial asset component. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VI (Depository Operations) covers Accredited Investors in the context of account opening. Series V-A covers them as mutual fund investors. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Three paths to accreditation: Income-only (Rs. 2 Cr), Net worth-only (Rs. 7.5 Cr with Rs. 3.75 Cr financial), Combo (Rs. 1 Cr income + Rs. 5 Cr net worth with Rs. 2.5 Cr financial). Body corporates need Rs. 50 Cr net worth.
